intro: Alibaba's alleged mass extraction of Claude's capabilities dominates the day; Bitcoin slides to multi-year lows on hot inflation data.

items.headline: Claude Code 2.1.191 Ships /rewind Command and Cuts Streaming CPU Use by 37%
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code 2.1.191 on June 25, adding /rewind to restore conversation context after a /clear, fixing background agents that restarted after being stopped, and coalescing streaming updates for roughly 37% CPU savings during long sessions.
items.why: The /rewind command directly addresses a common pain point in long agentic sessions; the CPU savings matter for laptop-based development.

items.headline: Engineering Roles Prove Most Resilient to AI Displacement, Now 55% of Big-Tech New Hires
items.summary: A SignalFire analysis tracking millions of employees found that while total hiring at major tech firms dropped 25% versus 2019, engineering fell only 11% and now comprises 55% of all new hires — up from 46% in 2019. Early-stage startups collectively hired 7% more engineers in 2025 than in 2019.
items.why: The data counters the displacement narrative: AI coding tools appear to be expanding engineering demand rather than shrinking it.

items.headline: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Running 28.8 Million-Query Distillation Attack Against Claude
items.summary: Anthropic told Senators Tim Scott and Elizabeth Warren that Alibaba's Qwen lab conducted 'the largest known distillation attack' on its models — using nearly 25,000 fraudulent accounts to extract Claude's software engineering and agentic reasoning capabilities via 28.8 million exchanges between April and June 2026. Anthropic called it a systematic effort to undermine U.S. AI export controls by a company already on the Pentagon's Chinese military companies list.
items.why: The largest publicly documented model-distillation attack against a U.S. AI lab, raising urgent questions about whether API access controls can meaningfully protect frontier model capabilities.

items.headline: U.S. Export Directive Forces Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Globally — Day 14
items.summary: The Trump administration's June 12 export-control directive ordered Anthropic to cut off all foreign nationals from Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing a jailbreak vulnerability in Mythos's cybersecurity capabilities. The suspension remains in force as of June 25, affecting all API customers; Anthropic publicly disputes the scope of the order.
items.why: The first time a U.S. government directive has forced a commercial AI lab to suspend its flagship models mid-deployment — and it's still live with no resolution timeline.

items.headline: OpenAI Expands GPT-5.5-Cyber to Vetted Defenders with Automated Vulnerability Patching
items.summary: OpenAI moved GPT-5.5-Cyber into broader availability for vetted critical-infrastructure defenders on June 22-23, pairing it with an upgraded Codex Security plugin and the open-source 'Patch the Planet' initiative with Trail of Bits. The model scores 85.6% on CyberGym and 69.8% on SEC-bench Pro.
items.why: Signals the field's shift toward specialized high-capability models — with dual-use implications now front-of-mind after the Fable/Mythos export ban.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot CLI's Redesigned Terminal Interface Reaches General Availability
items.summary: The new tabbed terminal UI for GitHub Copilot CLI — previewed at Microsoft Build 2026 — is now GA, adding a tabbed layout with Session, Gists, Issues, and Pull Requests panels, guided in-session tool configuration, and theme-aware accessible components. Update with `copilot update`.
items.why: A GA-stable agentic CLI that surfaces repo issues and PRs inline is a meaningful upgrade for developers living in the terminal.

items.headline: Visual Studio 2026 June Update Ships with Deep AI Platform Integration
items.summary: Microsoft released the Visual Studio 2026 June update on June 23, describing it as a 'new era' for the IDE with deep AI platform integration, improved performance, and stronger core fundamentals across the .NET and C++ toolchain.
items.why: The annual refresh for the IDE ecosystem used by millions of .NET and C++ developers now puts AI-assisted workflows front and center.

items.headline: GitHub Adds Break-Glass Credential Revocation for Enterprise Incident Response
items.summary: GitHub now lets enterprise owners instantly revoke SSO authorizations, personal access tokens, and SSH keys for any user — or across an entire enterprise — as a rapid incident-response measure, with self-service bulk revocation also available for individual members.
items.why: A long-requested security control; previously revoking credentials enterprise-wide required tedious per-account steps.

items.headline: Bitcoin Drops to $58,000 — Lowest Since September 2024 — as PCE Inflation Prints 4.1%
items.summary: U.S. PCE inflation rose 4.1% year-over-year in May — highest since April 2023 — triggering $1.26 billion in crypto liquidations on June 25. Bitcoin fell to a fresh 2026 low of $58,000 as the data extinguished hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts under Chair Kevin Warsh, who held the benchmark rate at 3.5–3.75%.
items.why: A PCE print double the Fed's 2% target, paired with a Fed chair who has already ruled out cuts, is the clearest macro driver for this leg down.

items.headline: Bitcoin Supply in Loss Hits All-Time High at 10.83 Million BTC
items.summary: Bitcoin's slide below $59,000 pushed a record 10.83 million BTC into unrealized loss on June 25, exceeding peaks seen at the bottoms of the 2019, 2020, and 2022 bear markets. Long-term holders controlling 14.8 million BTC are largely holding firm despite having more than a third of their stack underwater.
items.why: This metric has historically clustered near cycle bottoms; a new all-time high in supply-in-loss is a closely watched on-chain capitulation signal.

items.headline: Bitcoin ETF Assets Slide to $77.6B with $469M Single-Day Outflow, Back to Post-Election Lows
items.summary: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs shed $469 million in net outflows on June 24, led by BlackRock's IBIT at $239 million in redemptions. Total AUM has fallen from an October 2025 peak of $169.5 billion to $77.6 billion — erasing 19 months of gains and returning to levels seen just after Trump's November 2024 election victory.
items.why: $9 billion in cumulative net-inflow reversal from the peak signals institutional selling, not retail panic, as the dominant force in this drawdown.
